mnemonics.title: Cohen-Sutherland Decision Process
mnemonics.content: TRBL - Trivial Reject By Logic: If both endpoints share any clip region (AND ≠ 0), REJECT. If both endpoints are 0000, ACCEPT. Otherwise, SPLIT and compute intersection.

mnemonics.title: Outcode Bit Positions
mnemonics.content: TBRL - Top Bottom Right Left: Bits in order from MSB: Top=8, Bottom=4, Right=2, Left=1. Add values for outside regions to get outcode.

mnemonics.title: Liang-Barsky Parameter Logic
mnemonics.content: Entering u1 = max(0, qi/pi for pi<0), Exiting u2 = min(1, qi/pi for pi>0). If u1 > u2, line is outside. Otherwise clip from u1 to u2.

mnemonics.title: Sutherland-Hodgman Sequential Clipping
mnemonics.content: In-Out-In produces 2 points (intersection + in-point). In-In produces 2 points (both in). Out-Out produces 0 points. In-Out produces 1 point (intersection).

acronyms.usage: Line clipping algorithm using outcodes for trivial accept/reject
Parametric line clipping algorithm using inequalities
Polygon clipping algorithm processing one boundary at a time
General polygon clipping handling concave polygons and holes
Line clipping algorithm creating regions around clipping window for efficiency

visualTips.visualization: Draw a 3x3 grid representing 9 regions around the clipping rectangle. Label regions with their outcodes: center=0000, top=1000, bottom=0100, right=0010, left=0001. Corners and edges show combinations.
Represent the line as a segment from u=0 to u=1 on a number line. Show how boundaries shift u1 (entry) rightward and u2 (exit) leftward. The clipped segment is from u1 to u2.
Show polygon vertices being tested against each of 4 boundaries sequentially. Draw arrows showing where new intersection vertices are added. The polygon transforms gradually to fit inside.

analogies.analogy: Just as a camera's viewfinder shows only what falls within its frame, the clipping window defines the visible portion of the graphics world. Everything outside is not captured.
Like sorting mail: completely internal (0000) goes to accept pile, common outside region goes to reject pile, rest need individual processing at the boundary.
Clipping a polygon is like using a rectangular cookie cutter on dough - the cutter (window boundary) removes everything outside, leaving only the portion within.
The parametric u is like measuring along the line from start to end. Each boundary tells you where you enter (u1) and exit (u2) - clip to this measured segment.
